description: Powers of influence over mind and will — charming creatures, warping perception, countering spells, and bending the thoughts of others.
abilities: Aura's Silvery Broadcast

Broadcasting a brief spoken message that every creature across a wide area hears clearly in a voice that sounds pleasant to them. The sound seems to originate from every direction at once, and background noise is muted for the duration.
Aurora

Conjuring a dazzling prismatic aurora across the sky, the caster's eyes glittering like dancing wisps. The aurora is visible across a vast distance and persists for several minutes. Common folk who glimpse it are transfixed, stopping whatever they are doing to stare. Lesser foes are similarly dazzled, though a blow frees them from the trance. Powerful enemies may resist, but even they risk being staggered or stunned by the spectacle.
Bamboozle

Making a nearby creature mildly confused about a specific subject until the caster leaves the area. A merchant might become confused about the value of their goods, a guard about who is authorized to pass, or a dog about whether it is, in fact, a good boy. The confusion lifts if the deception is pushed too far.
Befizzle

Cursing a nearby creature and restricting its use of magic for the next hour. If the target attempts to cast a spell, it finds it impossible: spoken spells are forgotten mid-sentence, gestured spells freeze the limbs. When used against powerful enemies, the caster must maintain concentration to sustain the effect, and the curse ends if the caster moves or takes another action.
Control

Dominating the mind of a nearby creature for the next hour, compelling it to obey any verbal command without exception or hesitation. When the spell ends, the target remembers everything. This spell is cursed: if used to harm another creature, the caster permanently forfeits their soul to darkness and becomes a villain consumed by the magic's corruption.
Enscroll

Inscribing a known spell onto a scroll that anyone can activate by reading its words aloud. The process requires quiet concentration and additional effort proportional to the power of the inscribed spell. You choose what is written on the scroll.
Eternal Slumber

Placing a creature into permanent, peaceful sleep through touch. The caster may bind the enchantment to a specific trigger that will break it, such as a loved one's embrace or the destruction of a particular object. The spell can be ended at will, but may only be sustained on one creature at a time. Only works against powerful enemies if they are at death's door.
False Sense

Touching a creature and warping its perception of a specific thing or category of things. The town mayor might appear as a clown, all wolves might look like puppies, or fresh bread might smell like smoke. The illusion persists until the caster cancels it or the creature is harmed by the deception.
Fear

Breaching the mind of a nearby creature to seek their worst fear. At full power, the fear is learned and the creature becomes permanently haunted by it in every waking moment, their personality slowly destroyed until they become unresponsive. A strong success reveals the fear and conjures its image in the target's mind, paralyzing them with dread for several minutes. A partial success reveals the fear but the target ejects the caster before the vision can take hold. On failure, the caster learns the fear but is paralyzed by it instead, unable to use abilities for several minutes. A catastrophic failure permanently haunts the caster with the creature's fear, inflicting a lasting flaw that can only be lifted by the Liberate spell.
Forget

Erasing a sleeping creature's memories through touch, wiping up to a month of recollection. With additional effort, the erasure can be extended dramatically, stripping away years of memory for each measure of power invested.
Interpret

Entering the mind of a nearby creature and temporarily learning their language for a short time. With enough effort and creative communication, the language can be permanently absorbed into the caster's memory.
Invasion

Touching a sleeping creature and entering its dream, potentially bringing willing nearby companions along. Those who enter the dream fall into a trance, unable to sense anything outside, even harm to their bodies. The caster chooses how the party appears and may impersonate others within the dreamworld. The dream unfolds in two phases: first, a scene of the caster's design where the host is met and a suggestion is made, attempting to convince them of something, perhaps to abandon a pursuit, sever ties, or relinquish wealth. Then, the host's greatest fear manifests as a monster whose power is proportionate to the severity of the suggestion. If the monster is defeated, the host wakes believing the dream was a revelation and sets out to follow the suggestion. If the monster prevails, everyone wakes and the spell ends. Abilities may be used inside the dream, but their effects are purely fictional and cannot leave the dreamworld.
Lifelink

Entwining the life-forces of two nearby creatures through one of three mystical bonds. A two-way link causes damage dealt to either creature to echo in the other. A one-way link channels pain from one chosen creature to the other. A leyline mirrors healing instead, so that restorative energy flowing into one mends the other as well.
Mesmerize

Dazzling a nearby lesser foe or ordinary person with an optical illusion so captivating that the target cannot move, act, or respond to conversation until the caster leaves the area. The target must be able to see, and the spell ends if the creature is harmed.
Message

Reaching across distance to establish a telepathic link with a creature met before, allowing a brief exchange of thoughts. The caster must know the target's general location, though at greater power the link can be forged regardless of where the creature may be.
Modulate

Altering a nearby creature's physical sensations for up to an hour. Temperature can be shifted to soothing warmth, refreshing coolness, or oppressive extremes. Nourishment can be manipulated to make food seem revolting, to induce insatiable hunger, or to create a feeling of perfect satiation. Comfort can be granted as deep relaxation or denied as persistent restlessness.
No

Uttering the word "no" to attempt neutralizing a spell being cast nearby, declared before the spell takes effect. At best the spell is violently canceled and its caster suffers a backlash. More commonly the spell is simply stopped. The attempt may go awry — deflecting the spell onto an unintended target, failing to stop it entirely, or in the worst case accidentally amplifying the enemy's magic.
Nox

Infusing a creature through touch with a cocktail of sensations lasting up to an hour. The caster selects from effects such as wakefulness, heightened alertness, surging confidence, euphoria, uncontrollable laughter, vivid sensory perception, deep relaxation, sweating, nausea, chills, or sharp irritability.
Overthere

Making a suggestive gesture that redirects a nearby hostile creature's anger toward a different adversary. The target must have another enemy nearby for the spell to take effect, and the redirection is canceled if the caster harms the creature.
Perky Profanation

Cursing a nearby creature with an irresistible compulsion, choosing one of three effects: laughter, doubling them over in helpless mirth where everything sounds hilarious; dance, filling their ears with a favorite tune that compels them to dance as if their life depends on it; or mimicry, making them behave like a creature of the caster's choosing, such as a housecat. The curse is lifted if the target is harmed. At greater power, the effect cascades outward to affect any number of common folk in the area.
Reflect

Producing an instant counterspell that reflects a spell targeting you back toward its caster. Declared the moment you are targeted, the reflection can send the spell crashing back into the caster, deflect it harmlessly away, or — if the reflection wavers — redirect the spell into a nearby ally instead.
Shape Senses

Manipulating a creature's perception of color through touch: swapping hues, oversaturating or muting the spectrum, or imposing monochromatic vision. Any number of changes may be layered as long as they do not contradict one another. With greater effort, one of a lesser foe's senses can be transported to a random corner of the cosmos for a full day, leaving them to perceive an alien world through that sense alone.
Sigil

Drawing a magic sigil on an object, choosing a specific creature or type of creature it will affect and selecting one of four effects: lure, attracting the chosen creatures to the sigil's location where they linger briefly; repel, discouraging creatures from approaching; alert, sending a mental signal when creatures pass by; or message, telepathically transmitting a short phrase to creatures who come near. The spell lasts until canceled or recast, and can only be placed when no enemies are currently nearby. The sigil's design may be reused with a different effect each time.
Sleep

Whispering a soft lullaby that lulls nearby common folk into a dreamful slumber lasting up to an hour. Those affected collapse gently and sleep peacefully, though any harm breaks the enchantment instantly. With greater effort, the spell intensifies to overcome the willpower of lesser foes as well.
Speak

Speaking silently into the mind of a nearby creature. Your lips move as though forming words, but no sound escapes; instead, the target hears your voice inside their head and knows that the words originate from within their own mind.
Spellsteal

Briefly entering the mind of a nearby creature after observing them cast a spell, glimpsing its nature well enough to reproduce it once. The stolen knowledge is imperfect — the full effects and consequences may not be entirely clear. Once the stolen spell is cast or inscribed onto a scroll, the knowledge fades and cannot be used again.
Whisper

Telepathically sending a message to a nearby creature, heard as if spoken aloud though the target knows it comes from within their mind. At greater power, a two-way link is established, allowing a brief telepathic conversation.
Wren's Delightful Dream

Whispering and waving a hand at a nearby creature, enchanting their next slumber. When the creature falls asleep, they experience a deeply comforting dream and awaken feeling beloved, all grudges forgotten for the next week.
